Text: Hi [INSERT SUPPLIER NAME], Thank you for reaching out regarding your lead time update request. Lead times directly impact the delivery estimates customers see on site, so we need some additional information before making any changes. Wayfair’s expectation for non-Made to Order products is: Small Parcel: shipped within 1 business day Large Parcel: ready for pickup within 2 business days (NA) or 1 business day (EU) If you cannot meet this expectation, please provide: The business reason for the change and why the current lead time is no longer sufficient Any operational constraints impacting fulfillment (e.g. warehouse processing, staffing, or carrier pickup limitations) Updated lead times for: Large Parcel: Small Parcel: Confirmation that the updated lead times align with carrier collection schedules Whether this is a temporary or permanent change (and timeframe if temporary) Please also note that submitting an ASN or marking an order as shipped does not confirm carrier induction. Orders may still appear overdue until the first carrier scan is received. See New Dropship Order Status Changes article on Partner Home for more information . If you cannot meet the Must Ship By Date on an order, please submit an order delay via: Primary Reason: Order Not Processed On Time > Expected Ship Date > Submit Thank you, [AGENT NAME]

Text: Hi [INSERT SUPPLIER NAME], Thank you for providing the reason behind your request. We have submitted your request to our internal team for approval. The average turnaround time for these requests is 5 business days. If your lead time(s) have not changed after this time period, please follow up on this thread. Please backorder the order(s) in the meantime . You can tell if your lead time has changed through Must Ship by Date, as it is calculated using lead time. For reference, your cutoff time is [INSERT Cutoff Times] . Here is how we calculate Must Ship by Date: If the order is placed after cutoffs time, Must Ship by Date = Order Date + Lead Time If the order is placed before cutoffs time, Must Ship by Date = Order Date + Lead Time - 1 day If you have any remaining questions on this topic, please respond directly to this email and we will be happy to help. If you need assistance with another topic, please reach out to supplierservicedesk@wayfair.com to create a new ticket. Help us improve by sharing your feedback. You will receive an email shortly with a survey, or you can provide feedback on any closed tickets in Partner Home. We sincerely value your partnership. Thank you, [INSERT ASSOCIATE OR AGENT NAME]

Hi [INSERT SUPPLIER NAME], Thank you for reaching out. I'm happy to address this for you today. The ship badge is assigned automatically. There are 2 factors that decide delivery promise time on site: lead time and transit time. Transit time depends on the location of the customer. Therefore, your products might be eligible for a location but not for others. In order to be eligible for a speed badge on site, orders from your warehouse(s) must constantly and consistently ship the same-day and deliver on-time to customers. Dropship Requirements - Overall Small Parcel Performance Accurate child-level inventory feeds Same-day shipping on all orders placed prior to your warehouse cutoff time 98% on-time fulfillment for all orders 94% on-time delivery for all orders. Best practices to meet our same day requirements: Download orders multiple times per day Arrange for later pick up with FedEx Pull orders through 2:30pm to be shipped same day Weekend or additional shifts Multiple pickups during the day Prioritize Wayfair orders Hourly inventory feeds Note: We do not want you to send inventory more frequently than it is being updated. For items with delivery estimates that are between 3 - 5 days (US Only) away, "Fast Delivery" badge will be attached to the product. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to reach out. Help us improve by sharing your feedback. You will receive an email shortly with a survey, or you can provide feedback on any closed tickets in Partner Home. We sincerely value your partnership. Thank you, [INSERT ASSOCIATE OR AGENT NAME]

Hi [INSERT SUPPLIER NAME], Thank you for reaching out. I'm happy to address this for you today. Below is the difference between the lead time and the on-site delivery promise. The Lead Time is set in the system by the supplier or by a Wayfair employee and is always counted in business days (5 days a week - Monday through Friday), while the Delivery Promise displayed on site is a customer-facing value expressed in calendar days (7 days a week - Monday through Sunday) . This is why the Delivery Promise doesn’t equal the Lead Time in the system. Please see the below example to understand the difference between Lead Time to Delivery Promise: 480 hours Lead Time = 20 business days = 28 calendar days (weekends included) = Delivery Promise of 4 weeks. Moreover, additional time pads are applied to prevent any unforeseen problem from delaying the delivery promise. Supplier Lead Time Pads are additional time pads applied on top of the base lead time (in 24h increments), used to adjust Lead Time per day of the week & are applied based on order date/day of week. Carrier Lead Time Pads are cushions set to bridge the gap between base lead time and actual performance covering 95% of shipped orders. They are updated once every two weeks and cannot be set up on a SKU level. Thanks again for reaching out. I am marking this ticket as complete. If you need further support with this issue, please comment to re-open. Help us improve by sharing your feedback. You will receive an email shortly with a survey, or you can provide feedback on any closed tickets in Partner Home. We sincerely value your partnership. Thank you, [INSERT ASSOCIATE OR AGENT NAME]

Hi [INSERT SUPPLIER NAME], Thank you for reaching out. I'm happy to address this for you today. Replacement part lead times are centrally managed, and we have defined shipping expectations for acknowledging and shipping replacement parts within established service levels. Our current SLAs can be found on this help center article: https://partners.wayfair.com/help/2/article/207 If any replacement parts can be shipped within our backorder SLA: You must delay the replacement part as "Part is out of stock". The date picker will allow you to select any date within our backorder SLA. If replacement parts cannot be shipped within our SLAs, you must complete one of the following two actions: Backorder the replacement part as "discontinued". This will cancel the replacement part & Wayfair will offer the customer an alternate resolution. However, the item must be removed from our website before this option is available. If the item is not discontinued, but a replacement part cannot be shipped within SLA, you must backorder the replacement part as "whole unit needed". This will cancel the replacement part & order a replacement unit for the customer. Our Partner Home Help Center offers further information on replacement parts and the service levels, please see this article: https://partners.wayfair.com/help/2/article/2883 Thanks again for reaching out. This ticket is now marked as complete. If you do need further support with this issue, please add a comment to re-open. Help us improve by sharing your feedback. You will receive an email shortly with a survey, or you can provide feedback on any closed tickets in Partner Home. We sincerely value your partnership. Kind Regards, [INSERT ASSOCIATE OR AGENT NAME]
